provenance.source_corpus: Incomplete recovery of plant diversity in restored prairie wetlands on agricultural landscapes

source_spans.text: species richness is lowest in drained wetlands, increases rapidly within 5 years of restoration, and appears to stabilize within 6–10 years of restoration
species richness is lowest in drained wetlands, increases rapidly within 5 years of restoration, and appears to stabilize within 6–10 years of restoration
Wetland area was positively correlated with species richness (r=0.474, p=0.009)
wetland slope was positively correlated with percent cover of non-native species (r=0.456, p=0.013)
wetland slope was positively correlated with percent cover of non-native species (r=0.456, p=0.013)
large-scale loss of wetlands from these landscapes has resulted in scarce or depauperate native seed banks and plant propagules
large-scale loss of wetlands from these landscapes has resulted in scarce or depauperate native seed banks and plant propagules
Key features of invasive species... provide an advantage in newly disturbed habitats for these species to establish quickly and efficiently, thereby impeding establishment of native hydrophytes
